<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1" />
<title>The Atiyah–Singer index theorem</title>
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.css">
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.js"></script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/contrib/auto-render.min.js"></script>
<script>
(function(){
  function renderAll(){
    if(typeof renderMathInElement!=='function'){ return setTimeout(renderAll,50); }
    renderMathInElement(document.body,{
      delimiters:[
        {left:'$$',right:'$$',display:true},
        {left:'$',right:'$',display:false},
        {left:'\\(',right:'\\)',display:false},
        {left:'\\[',right:'\\]',display:true}

        '\\Hom':'\\Hom',

        '\\ad':'\\ad',

      }
    });
  }
  if(document.readyState==='loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded',renderAll);
  else renderAll();
})();
</script>
<style>
  :root{
    --bg:#0f1218; --panel:#161b24; --panel2:#1c2230;
    --ink:#e8ecf1; --mute:#9aa4b2; --line:#2a3242;
    --yellow:#ffd866; --blue:#58c4dd; --green:#83c167;
    --pink:#e07a5f; --violet:#b39ddb; --cyan:#7de0d6;
  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box}
  html,body{margin:0;padding:0;background:var(--bg);color:var(--ink);
    font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Segoe UI",Inter,Roboto,sans-serif;
    font-size:17px;line-height:1.65}
  a{color:var(--blue)}
  code{font-family:ui-monospace,SFMono-Regular,Menlo,Consolas,monospace;
    background:#0b0f16;padding:1px 6px;border-radius:4px;color:#f0c987;font-size:0.92em}
  main{max-width:880px;margin:0 auto;padding:4rem 1.5rem 8rem}
  h1{font-size:2.4rem;letter-spacing:-0.01em;margin:0 0 0.4rem}
  h2{font-size:1.6rem;margin:3.2rem 0 0.8rem;color:#fff;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding-bottom:.4rem}
  h3{font-size:1.15rem;margin:1.8rem 0 .6rem;color:var(--yellow)}
  p{margin:0.7rem 0}
  .sub{color:var(--mute);margin-top:0;margin-bottom:2rem}
  .widget{background:var(--panel);border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:10px;
    padding:1rem 1.1rem 1.2rem;margin:1.2rem 0}
  .widget .hd{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:baseline;margin-bottom:.6rem;gap:.8rem;flex-wrap:wrap}
  .widget .hd .ttl{font-weight:600;color:#fff;font-size:.95rem;letter-spacing:.02em;text-transform:uppercase}
  .widget .hd .hint{color:var(--mute);font-size:.85rem}
  .row{display:flex;gap:.6rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;margin:.4rem 0}
  .row label{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem}
  input[type="text"],input[type="number"],select{
    background:#0b0f16;color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .55rem;font-family:inherit;font-size:.95rem}
  input[type="range"]{accent-color:var(--yellow);flex:1;max-width:260px}
  button{background:var(--panel2);color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .7rem;cursor:pointer;font:inherit}
  button:hover{background:#242c3e}
  button.active{background:var(--yellow);color:#111;border-color:var(--yellow)}
  .readout{background:#0b0f16;border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:6px;
    padding:.6rem .8rem;font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;font-size:.92rem;color:#cbd5e1;
    min-height:1.6em;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-word}
  .pill{display:inline-block;background:var(--panel2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:999px;padding:1px 8px;font-size:.82rem;color:var(--mute);margin-right:4px}
  .note{border-left:3px solid var(--violet);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d8d2ef;background:#1a1828;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .ok{border-left:3px solid var(--green);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d5ebc8;background:#1a2418;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .bad{border-left:3px solid var(--pink);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#f4d2c7;background:#2a1a18;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  svg{display:block;margin:0 auto;max-width:100%}
  svg text{fill:var(--ink);font-family:inherit}
  nav.toc{position:sticky;top:0;background:rgba(15,18,24,.93);backdrop-filter:blur(8px);
    border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.6rem 1rem;z-index:10;
    display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:.9rem}
  nav.toc a{color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none}
  nav.toc a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}
  /* sticky sidebar TOC (wide screens only; narrow screens fall back to top-nav) */
  aside.sidetoc{position:fixed;top:4.2rem;
    left:max(1rem, calc(50% - 440px - 220px));
    width:200px;max-height:calc(100vh - 6rem);overflow-y:auto;
    padding-right:1rem;border-right:1px solid var(--line);
    font-size:.88rem;z-index:5}
  aside.sidetoc .sidetoc-title{font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute);margin:0 0 .5rem;padding-bottom:.35rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line)}
  aside.sidetoc ol{list-style:none;padding:0;margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc li{margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc a{display:block;padding:.3rem .5rem;color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none;
    border-left:2px solid transparent;line-height:1.35;
    transition:color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease}
  aside.sidetoc a:hover{color:var(--ink)}
  aside.sidetoc a.active{color:var(--yellow);border-left-color:var(--yellow)}
  @media (max-width:1180px){ aside.sidetoc{display:none} }
  @media (min-width:1181px){ nav.toc a[href^="#"]{display:none} }
  .hero{padding:3rem 0 1rem}
  .small{font-size:.88rem;color:var(--mute)}
  footer{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem;text-align:center;margin-top:4rem}
  table.plain{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:.95rem;margin:.6rem 0}
  table.plain th,table.plain td{padding:.35rem .5rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);text-align:left}
  table.plain th{color:var(--mute);font-weight:500}
  /* callback-css-auto-begin */
  aside.callback{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:rgba(88,196,221,0.05);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--cyan) 45%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.callback .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.callback ul{margin:0;padding-left:1.1rem}
  aside.callback ul li{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.callback a{color:inherit}
/* callback-css-auto-end */
  details.changelog{
    margin:2rem 0 1rem;padding:.6rem .9rem;
    background:rgba(0,0,0,0.22);
    border-radius:6px;
    font-size:.85rem;
  }
  details.changelog summary{
    cursor:pointer;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:.7rem;color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);
  }
  details.changelog table{margin-top:.5rem;border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}
  details.changelog td{padding:.2rem .6rem;border-bottom:1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.07);vertical-align:top}
  details.changelog td:first-child{color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  aside.related{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 6%, transparent);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 55%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.related .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.related > div{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.related .more{color:var(--mute);font-style:italic}
  aside.related a{color:inherit}
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-begin */
  html[data-hide-widgets] .widget { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-quizzes] .quiz { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-lineage] .lineage-strip { display: none !important; }
  .mv-display-toggles { display: inline-flex; gap: .6rem; align-items: center; }
  .mv-display-toggle { background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--line, #333); border-radius: 6px; padding: 5px 10px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1; color: inherit; transition: background 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease; }
  .mv-display-toggle:hover { background: var(--panel, rgba(255,255,255,0.06)); border-color: var(--mute, #666); }
  .mv-display-toggle--off { opacity: 0.35; }
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-end */
</style>

The Atiyah–Singer index theorem

An elliptic operator $D$ on a closed manifold has two integers attached to it: the analytic index $\ind(D)=\dim\ker D-\dim\mathrm{coker}\,D$, computed by hard PDE; and the topological index, computed from the K-theory class of its symbol. Atiyah–Singer (1963) says the two agree, and the resulting cohomological pairing $\int_M\mathrm{ch}\cdot\mathrm{Td}$ packages Gauss–Bonnet, Hirzebruch–Riemann–Roch, the signature theorem, and the $\hat A$-genus into a single equation.

blocks.html:
1. The index theorem

Let $M$ be a closed (compact, no boundary) smooth manifold and $E,F\to M$ smooth complex vector bundles. A linear differential operator $D\colon\Gamma(E)\to\Gamma(F)$ is elliptic if its principal symbol is invertible off the zero section of $T^*M$ (made precise in §2). On a closed manifold, ellipticity forces $D$ to be Fredholm as a map between Sobolev completions, and so

$$\ind(D) \;=\; \dim\ker D \;-\; \dim\mathrm{coker}\,D \;\in\;\mathbb{Z}$$

is a finite integer. The miracle is that this analytic invariant is rigid: it only depends on the homotopy class of the principal symbol $\sigma(D)$, viewed as a K-theory class on $T^*M$.

The theorem (Atiyah–Singer 1963). For any elliptic $D$, $$\boxed{\;\ind(D) \;=\; \bigl\langle\,\mathrm{ch}\bigl([\sigma(D)]\bigr)\smile\mathrm{Td}\bigl(TM\otimes\mathbb{C}\bigr),\,[T^*M]\,\bigr\rangle.\;}$$ The left side is computed by counting solutions and obstructions of a PDE; the right side, by integrating universal characteristic-class polynomials over $T^*M$. Both sides are integers, and they agree.

What makes the equation interesting is the asymmetry between the two sides. The left side is genuinely about the operator: write down a different elliptic $D'$ with the same symbol class, and the kernel and cokernel will generally differ — but $\dim\ker-\dim\mathrm{coker}$ stays put. The right side is purely topological, mechanical to compute given the symbol and the topology of $M$. Atiyah–Singer is the bridge.

Index of a $1$-parameter family of $1\times 1$ operators
slide $\lambda$ — eigenvalues cross zero, $\dim\ker-\dim\mathrm{coker}$ stays fixed
$D_\lambda = -i\partial_\theta+\lambda$ on $S^1$. Sliding $\lambda$ shifts the eigenvalue lattice; eigenvalues cross zero, but the analytic index of the toy chiral operator $D=\partial_x+f(x,\lambda)$ stays fixed.
The integer $\ind(D)$ is therefore a homotopy invariant of the symbol — exactly the kind of object K-theory was built to measure.

See also
  • Chern–Weil theory
  • Applications: index theorem & K-theory of $\mathcal{O}_F$
  • Chern character

Used in
The Atiyah–Singer index theorem · Classical specialisations
The Atiyah–Singer index theorem · Dirac operators and the $\hat A$-genus
</section>

2. Elliptic operators and the Fredholm property

A linear differential operator of order $m$, locally $$D \;=\; \sum_{|\alpha|\le m} a_\alpha(x)\,\partial^\alpha,$$ has principal symbol $\sigma_m(D)(x,\xi)=\sum_{|\alpha|=m}a_\alpha(x)(i\xi)^\alpha$, a polynomial in $\xi\in T_x^*M$ of degree exactly $m$. Globally $\sigma_m(D)$ is a bundle map $\pi^*E\to\pi^*F$ over $T^*M$, where $\pi\colon T^*M\to M$.

$D$ is elliptic if $\sigma_m(D)(x,\xi)$ is invertible for every $\xi\ne 0$. Three running examples:

operatorsymbolelliptic?
Laplacian $-\Delta$ on $\mathbb{R}^n$$|\xi|^2$yes — vanishes only at $\xi=0$
heat operator $\partial_t-\Delta$ on $\mathbb{R}^{n+1}$$i\tau+|\xi|^2$no — vanishes when $\tau=0,\,\xi=0$ (parabolic)
Cauchy–Riemann $\bar\partial=\tfrac{1}{2}(\partial_x+i\partial_y)$$\tfrac{i}{2}(\xi_1+i\xi_2)$yes — non-zero off origin
Why ellipticity is Fredholm. Pseudodifferential calculus produces a parametrix $Q$ for any elliptic $D$: an operator with $QD=I-S$ and $DQ=I-T$ where $S,T$ are smoothing (and hence compact between any Sobolev spaces). Compactness of the error pieces is the technical core: $D\colon H^s(E)\to H^{s-m}(F)$ has finite-dimensional kernel, closed range with finite-codimensional cokernel, and so a well-defined index. Elliptic regularity then says $u\in\ker D\Rightarrow u\in C^\infty$ — kernels are smooth, not merely $L^2$.

Symbol of the Laplacian on $\mathbb{R}^2$
$\sigma_2(-\Delta)(\xi)=|\xi|^2$ — vanishes only at the origin
$-\Delta$: $\sigma_2(\xi)=\xi_1^2+\xi_2^2$ — vanishes only at the origin, invertible elsewhere. The zero set in the cotangent fibre is just $\{0\}$, the canonical signature of ellipticity.

See also
  • Classification of PDEs (elliptic / parabolic / hyperbolic)
  • Lax–Milgram and elliptic regularity
  • Spectral theorem (bounded self-adjoint)
</section>

3. The topological index via $\mathrm{ch}\cdot\mathrm{Td}$

The symbol of an elliptic operator is more than a function on $T^*M$: it is a bundle map $\sigma\colon\pi^*E\to\pi^*F$ that is an isomorphism over $T^*M\setminus 0$. Such data assemble into a compactly supported K-theory class $$[\sigma(D)] \;\in\; K^0_{\mathrm{c}}(T^*M).$$ Outside a compact subset of $T^*M$ the bundles $\pi^*E$ and $\pi^*F$ are identified by $\sigma$, so the difference $[\pi^*E]-[\pi^*F]$ is supported on a compact piece — exactly the data $K^0_{\mathrm{c}}$ records.

Once the symbol lives in K-theory, the topological index is constructed by a sequence of natural pushforwards. Choose a closed embedding $i\colon M\hookrightarrow\mathbb{R}^N$ (Whitney). Push forward $[\sigma(D)]$ first to $K^0_{\mathrm{c}}(T^*\mathbb{R}^N)\cong\mathbb{Z}$ via the K-theoretic Thom isomorphism, then read off the integer:

$$\ind_{\mathrm{top}}(D) \;=\; \int_{T^*M}\mathrm{ch}\bigl([\sigma(D)]\bigr)\cdot\mathrm{Td}\bigl(TM\otimes\mathbb{C}\bigr).$$

Why the Todd class. The K-theory pushforward and the ordinary-cohomology pushforward (integration of the Chern character) disagree. The discrepancy is the Todd genus of the normal bundle of the embedding, which after some manipulation reduces to $\mathrm{Td}(TM\otimes\mathbb{C})$. Demanding that the index be a homotopy invariant of $[\sigma(D)]$ alone forces this correction term — it is the K-theoretic Riemann–Roch defect.

The Todd polynomial in formal Chern roots $x_i$ of a complex bundle is $$\mathrm{Td}(E) \;=\; \prod_i \frac{x_i}{1-e^{-x_i}} \;=\; 1 + \tfrac{1}{2}c_1 + \tfrac{1}{12}(c_1^2 + c_2) + \tfrac{1}{24}c_1 c_2 + \cdots,$$ a power series in the Chern classes with rational coefficients. The first few are revealing: for a curve $X$, $\mathrm{Td}(TX)=1+\tfrac{1}{2}c_1(TX)$, and Riemann–Roch says $\chi(X,L)=\deg L+1-g$.

Components of $\mathrm{ch}(E)\cdot\mathrm{Td}(TM_\mathbb{C})$
choose dim and rank — the dimension-matching component is the index
$\dim_\mathbb{C} M=2$, rank $E=1$: integrand is $\bigl[\mathrm{ch}(E)\cdot\mathrm{Td}(TM_\mathbb{C})\bigr]_4 = \tfrac{1}{12}(c_1(TM)^2+c_2(TM)) + \tfrac{1}{2}c_1(L)\,c_1(TM) + \tfrac{1}{2}c_1(L)^2$. Pair with $[M]$ to get $\chi(X,L)$.

See also
  • Topological $K^0(X)$ from vector bundles
  • Grothendieck group $K_0$ of an exact category
  • Bott periodicity
  • Chern classes
  • Pontryagin classes
</section>

4. Classical specialisations

The point of bundling Gauss–Bonnet, Hirzebruch–Riemann–Roch, and the signature theorem under one umbrella is that all three pick a specific elliptic operator $D$ and read off the right-hand side of $\ind(D)=\int\mathrm{ch}\cdot\mathrm{Td}$.

$D$$\ind(D)$topological side
$d+d^*\colon\Omega^{\mathrm{ev}}\to\Omega^{\mathrm{odd}}$$\chi(M)$$\int_M e(TM)$ — Gauss–Bonnet–Chern
signature operator $D_+\colon\Omega^+\to\Omega^-$$\sigma(M)$$\int_M L(TM)$ — Hirzebruch signature
$\bar\partial_E\colon\Omega^{0,\mathrm{ev}}(E)\to\Omega^{0,\mathrm{odd}}(E)$$\chi(X,E)$$\int_X\mathrm{ch}(E)\cdot\mathrm{Td}(TX)$ — HRR
Dirac on a spin manifold$\hat A$-genus$\int_M\hat A(TM)$ — see §5
Gauss–Bonnet–Chern in dim 2. Take $M^2$ a closed oriented surface and $D=d+d^*$ between even and odd forms. Hodge theory gives $\dim\ker D=\dim H^0+\dim H^2$ and $\dim\mathrm{coker}\,D=\dim H^1$. So $\ind(D)=\chi(M)$, and the topological side is $\int_M e(TM)=\tfrac{1}{2\pi}\int_M K\,dA$. This is the classical Gauss–Bonnet theorem for surfaces.
Hirzebruch signature in dim 4. Take $M^4$ closed oriented; the signature operator on $\Omega^2_+\oplus\Omega^0\oplus\Omega^4$ has index $\sigma(M)$, the signature of the intersection form on $H^2$. The right side is $\int_M L_1(TM)=\tfrac{1}{3}\langle p_1(TM),[M]\rangle$, and so $\sigma(M)=\tfrac{1}{3}p_1[M]$. This forces $p_1$ to be divisible by $3$ on closed oriented $4$-manifolds.

Hirzebruch–Riemann–Roch deserves separate treatment: on a closed complex manifold $X$ with a holomorphic vector bundle $E\to X$, the Dolbeault operator $\bar\partial_E$ from $(0,\mathrm{even})$- to $(0,\mathrm{odd})$-forms has index equal to the holomorphic Euler characteristic $\chi(X,E)=\sum(-1)^k\dim H^k(X,E)$. Atiyah–Singer says $$\chi(X,E) \;=\; \int_X \mathrm{ch}(E)\cdot\mathrm{Td}(TX).$$ For $X=\mathbb{P}^1$ and $E=\mathcal{O}(d)$ this collapses to $\chi(\mathbb{P}^1,\mathcal{O}(d))=d+1$. For a curve of genus $g$ and a line bundle $L$ of degree $d$, $\chi(L)=d+1-g$ — Riemann–Roch.

Classical specialisations dictionary
click an operator — see what the index theorem says about it
$d+d^*$ on a closed oriented manifold: $\ind=\chi(M)$, integrand $=e(TM)$. In dim 2, $\chi=\tfrac{1}{2\pi}\int K\,dA$ — Gauss–Bonnet.

See also
  • Euler class

Used in

</section>

5. Dirac operators and the $\hat A$-genus

The Dirac operator is the cleanest setting for Atiyah–Singer. On a Riemannian spin manifold $M^{2n}$ the spinor bundle $S\to M$ splits as $S=S^+\oplus S^-$, and the Dirac operator $D\colon\Gamma(S^+)\to\Gamma(S^-)$ is built from the spin connection by $$D \;=\; \sum_i e_i\cdot\nabla_{e_i},$$ with $e_i\cdot$ acting by Clifford multiplication. Its principal symbol $\sigma(D)(x,\xi)=i\xi\cdot$ is invertible on $\xi\ne 0$ since Clifford multiplication squares to $-|\xi|^2$ — so $D$ is elliptic.

The Dirac index. Atiyah–Singer says $$\ind(D) \;=\; \int_M \hat A(TM), \qquad \hat A \;=\; \prod_i \frac{x_i/2}{\sinh(x_i/2)} \;=\; 1 - \tfrac{1}{24}p_1 + \tfrac{1}{5760}(7p_1^2 - 4p_2) + \cdots,$$ where $x_i$ are formal Pontryagin roots and $p_i$ are Pontryagin classes. On a closed spin $4$-manifold, $\ind(D)=-\langle p_1(TM),[M]\rangle/24$. By the signature theorem, this equals $-\sigma(M)/8$, recovering Rokhlin's $\sigma(M)\equiv 0\pmod{16}$ for spin $4$-manifolds.

The Dirac operator's spectrum encodes geometric information directly. On the round $n$-sphere with metric of curvature $1$, $D^2=\nabla^*\nabla+\tfrac{R}{4}$ (Lichnerowicz), and the eigenvalues are explicitly $$\lambda_k \;=\; \pm\bigl(k+\tfrac{n}{2}\bigr), \qquad k=0,1,2,\ldots,$$ with multiplicities given by representation-theoretic Weyl-dimension formulas. None of the eigenvalues are zero, so $\ker D=0$ and $\ind(D)=0$ — consistent with $\hat A(S^n)=0$ for $n\ge 1$.

Lichnerowicz vanishing. If $M$ is a closed spin manifold admitting a metric with strictly positive scalar curvature, then $\ker D=0$ and so $\hat A(M)=\ind(D)=0$. This is a sharp obstruction: the K3 surface has $\hat A=2$, hence carries no positive-scalar-curvature metric — even though $K3$ is simply connected and topologically tame.

Dirac spectrum on the round sphere
slide the radius — eigenvalues $\pm(k+n/2)/r$ rescale, but the index stays $0$
$D$ on $S^2$ at $r=1$: eigenvalues $\pm(k+1)$ for $k\ge 0$, multiplicities $2(k+1)$. Spectrum is symmetric about $0$, so $\dim\ker=0$ and $\ind(D)=0$, consistent with $\hat A(S^2)=0$.

See also
  • Pontryagin classes

Used in
The Atiyah–Singer index theorem · Heat kernel, families, equivariant, anomalies
</section>

6. Heat kernel, families, equivariant, anomalies

The heat-kernel proof

McKean–Singer (1967): for a Dirac-type operator $D$ on a closed manifold, $$\ind(D) \;=\; \tr\bigl(e^{-tD^*D}\bigr) - \tr\bigl(e^{-tDD^*}\bigr), \qquad \forall\,t>0.$$ The right-hand side is independent of $t$ because non-zero eigenvalues of $D^*D$ pair with equal eigenvalues of $DD^*$ (via $u\mapsto Du/\|Du\|$) and contribute opposite signs that cancel. Sending $t\to 0$, the heat-kernel asymptotic $$K_{D^2}(t,x,x) \;\sim\; \sum_{k\ge 0} t^{(k-n)/2}\,a_k(x)$$ produces a local index density $a_n(x)$ whose integral over $M$ gives $\ind(D)$. After cancellation of the lower-order coefficients (a miraculous algebraic identity — Getzler's symbol calculus is the cleanest derivation), what survives is exactly $\hat A(TM)\,\mathrm{ch}(E/S)$.

The families index theorem

Now let $D=\{D_b\}_{b\in B}$ be a family of elliptic operators, smoothly parametrised by $b$ ranging over a compact base $B$. Naively the kernels and cokernels at different $b$ are vector spaces of varying dimension. After a small perturbation they assemble into a virtual bundle on $B$: $$\ind_{\text{fam}}(D) \;=\; [\ker D] - [\mathrm{coker}\,D] \;\in\; K^0(B).$$ The families index theorem of Atiyah–Singer (1971): this class is computed by a fibre integration of $\mathrm{ch}\cdot\mathrm{Td}$ in cohomology of the total space. When $B=\mathrm{pt}$ this collapses to the single-operator theorem.

Equivariant index

If a compact Lie group $G$ acts on $M$ and the operator $D$ is $G$-equivariant, $\ker D$ and $\mathrm{coker}\,D$ are finite-dimensional $G$-representations. The $G$-equivariant index $$\ind_G(D) \;\in\; R(G)$$ lives in the representation ring of $G$. Atiyah–Bott's fixed-point formula computes its character at $g\in G$ as a sum of contributions localised at the fixed-point set $M^g$.

Anomalies and instantons

In gauge theory on a closed $4$-manifold $M$, the Dirac operator coupled to a gauge connection $A$ on a vector bundle $V\to M$ has index $$\ind(D_A) \;=\; \int_M \hat A(TM)\,\mathrm{ch}(V) \;=\; \int_M c_2(V) - \tfrac{1}{24}\langle p_1(TM),[M]\rangle\,\mathrm{rk}(V) + \cdots.$$ The leading $\int c_2(V)$ is the instanton number — the topological charge of the gauge field, classifying $\mathrm{SU}(N)$ bundles up to isomorphism. The chiral anomaly in physics literature is exactly the statement that $\ind(D_A)$ does not vanish: the symmetry between left- and right-handed fermions is broken by the topology of the gauge bundle, by an integer the index theorem computes.

Anomaly diagram: index as topological charge
slide instanton number $k$ — the chiral imbalance in the Dirac spectrum tracks $k$
$k=1$: a single instanton on $M^4$ contributes one zero-mode of $D_A^+$ over $D_A^-$, so $\ind(D_A)=+1=\int_M c_2(V)$. The chiral anomaly: $\partial_\mu j_5^\mu = -\tfrac{1}{8\pi^2}\tr(F\wedge F)$ integrates to this index.
</section>

Connections

Atiyah–Singer is a hub theorem: it pulls together K-theory, characteristic classes, elliptic PDE, spin geometry, and gauge-theoretic physics into one accountancy. The frontier is broad — equivariant K-theory of loop spaces (Witten genus), index theory on non-compact and singular spaces (Atiyah–Patodi–Singer eta invariants and APS boundary conditions), $L^2$-index theorems for covering spaces (Atiyah–Connes), and the recent NCG / coarse-index programme (Connes–Higson–Roe) where Atiyah–Singer becomes a special case of an index in $K$-homology paired against $K$-theory.

  • K-theory — applications — supplies the topological-index home $K^0(T^*M)$ and the Bott periodicity that makes the construction natural.
  • Chern–Weil theory — provides the de Rham representatives $\mathrm{ch}$ and $\mathrm{Td}$ that get integrated.
  • PDE — elliptic regularity — the analytic underpinning that makes $\dim\ker$ and $\dim\mathrm{coker}$ finite in the first place.
  • Differential geometry — connections, curvature, and spin structures are the inputs to the Dirac operator.
  • Spectral theory — Fredholm-operator framework on Hilbert spaces, the abstract setting in which the index is defined.

/* sidebar TOC builder + active-section highlighting */
(function(){
  function build(){
    const aside = document.querySelector("aside.sidetoc");
    const topnav = document.querySelector("nav.toc");
    if(!aside || !topnav) return;
    const srcs = [...topnav.querySelectorAll('a[href^="#"]')];
    if(!srcs.length) return;
    const title = document.createElement("div");
    title.className = "sidetoc-title";
    title.textContent = "On this page";
    aside.appendChild(title);
    const ol = document.createElement("ol");
    srcs.forEach(src => {
      const li = document.createElement("li");
      const a = document.createElement("a");
      a.href = src.getAttribute("href");
      a.textContent = src.textContent.replace(/ /g," ").trim();
      li.appendChild(a); ol.appendChild(li);
    });
    aside.appendChild(ol);
    const links = [...aside.querySelectorAll("a")];
    const sections = [];
    links.forEach(a => {
      const id = a.getAttribute("href").slice(1);
      const s = document.getElementById(id);
      if(s) sections.push({a, s});
    });
    if(!sections.length) return;
    function update(){
      const threshold = 140;
      let activeLink = sections[0].a;
      for(const {a,s} of sections){
        if(s.getBoundingClientRect().top <= threshold) activeLink = a;
      }
      links.forEach(x => x.classList.remove("active"));
      activeLink.classList.add("active");
    }
    update();
    window.addEventListener("scroll", update, {passive:true});
    window.addEventListener("resize", update);
  }
  if(document.readyState === "loading") document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", build);
  else build();
})();
</script>

/* =============================== SECTION 1: Index of a 1-parameter family =============================== */
(function(){
  const svg = $("#ix-svg");
  if(!svg) return;
  const W = 820, H = 280;
  const lam = $("#ix-lam");
  const readout = $("#ix-readout");

  function draw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const cx = 410, cy = 140;
    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:60,y1:cy,x2:760,y2:cy,stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":1}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:cx,y1:30,x2:cx,y2:250,stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":1}));
    const tax = SVG("text",{x:760,y:cy-6,"font-size":12,fill:"var(--mute)","text-anchor":"end"});
    tax.textContent = "k (mode)";
    svg.appendChild(tax);
    const tay = SVG("text",{x:cx+8,y:36,"font-size":12,fill:"var(--mute)"});
    tay.textContent = "eigenvalue";
    svg.appendChild(tay);
    // zero line label
    const tz = SVG("text",{x:50,y:cy+4,"font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)"});
    tz.textContent = "0";
    svg.appendChild(tz);

    const L = parseFloat(lam.value);
    const scale = 35; // pixels per unit
    let cross = 0;
    for(let k=-7;k<=7;k++){
      const ev = k + L; // eigenvalues of -i d/dθ + λ on S^1
      const x = cx + k*40;
      const y = cy - ev*scale;
      // draw eigenvalue dot
      const fill = ev > 0 ? "var(--cyan)" : (ev < 0 ? "var(--pink)" : "var(--yellow)");
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:x,cy:y,r:4,fill,stroke:"none"}));
      // labels
      if(Math.abs(k)<=3){
        const lab = SVG("text",{x:x,y:cy+18,"font-size":10,fill:"var(--mute)","text-anchor":"middle"});
        lab.textContent = String(k);
        svg.appendChild(lab);
      }
    }

    // index of toy chiral operator: round(L) gives # of crossings
    const ind = Math.round(L);
    // chiral readout box
    const box = SVG("rect",{x:600,y:40,width:170,height:60,rx:8,fill:"var(--panel2)",stroke:"var(--yellow)"});
    svg.appendChild(box);
    const lbl = SVG("text",{x:685,y:62,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":13,fill:"var(--mute)"});
    lbl.textContent = "spectral flow";
    svg.appendChild(lbl);
    const val = SVG("text",{x:685,y:88,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":18,fill:"var(--yellow)","font-weight":600});
    val.textContent = "ind = " + (ind>=0 ? "+" : "") + ind;
    svg.appendChild(val);
  }

  function update(){
    draw();
    const L = parseFloat(lam.value);
    const ind = Math.round(L);
    readout.textContent = `D_λ = -i ∂_θ + λ on S¹, λ=${L.toFixed(2)}. Eigenvalues k+λ shift; net crossings of 0 (spectral flow) = ${ind}. The chiral toy operator has index = ${ind} — note how it stays integer-valued under continuous deformation.`;
  }
  lam.addEventListener("input", update);
  update();
})();

/* =============================== SECTION 2: Symbol of operators =============================== */
(function(){
  const svg = $("#sy-svg");
  if(!svg) return;
  const buttons = $$(".sy-pick");
  const readout = $("#sy-readout");
  let current = "laplace";

  function ops(key, x, y){
    if(key === "laplace") return x*x + y*y;
    if(key === "dbar") return Math.hypot(x, y); // |ξ|
    if(key === "wave") return -x*x + y*y; // τ²-|ξ|² lookalike, sign indicates lightcone
    return 0;
  }

  function draw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const W=820, H=280;
    const cx = 280, cy = 140, R = 110;
    // heat-map grid sample
    const N = 32;
    const dx = (2*R)/N;
    let maxV = 0;
    const grid = [];
    for(let i=0;i<N;i++){
      const row = [];
      for(let j=0;j<N;j++){
        const xi = -R + (i+0.5)*dx;
        const yi = -R + (j+0.5)*dx;
        const v = ops(current, xi/40, yi/40);
        row.push(v);
        if(Math.abs(v)>maxV) maxV = Math.abs(v);
      }
      grid.push(row);
    }
    // plot
    for(let i=0;i<N;i++){
      for(let j=0;j<N;j++){
        const v = grid[i][j];
        const t = maxV>0 ? v/maxV : 0;
        let fill;
        if(current === "wave"){
          fill = t > 0 ? `rgba(125,224,214,${0.15+0.6*Math.abs(t)})` : `rgba(224,122,95,${0.15+0.6*Math.abs(t)})`;
        } else {
          const a = 0.1 + 0.7*Math.abs(t);
          fill = `rgba(255,216,102,${a})`;
        }
        svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{
          x: cx-R + i*dx, y: cy-R + j*dx, width: dx+0.5, height: dx+0.5,
          fill, stroke: "none"
        }));
      }
    }
    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:cx-R,y1:cy,x2:cx+R,y2:cy,stroke:"var(--mute)","stroke-width":1,opacity:0.5}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:cx,y1:cy-R,x2:cx,y2:cy+R,stroke:"var(--mute)","stroke-width":1,opacity:0.5}));
    const lab1 = SVG("text",{x:cx+R+6,y:cy+4,"font-size":12,fill:"var(--mute)"});
    lab1.textContent = "ξ₁";
    svg.appendChild(lab1);
    const lab2 = SVG("text",{x:cx-4,y:cy-R-6,"font-size":12,fill:"var(--mute)","text-anchor":"end"});
    lab2.textContent = "ξ₂";
    svg.appendChild(lab2);

    // zero set marker
    if(current === "laplace" || current === "dbar"){
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx,cy,r:4,fill:"var(--pink)",stroke:"none"}));
      const t = SVG("text",{x:cx,y:cy+22,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":11,fill:"var(--pink)"});
      t.textContent = "zero set: {0}";
      svg.appendChild(t);
    } else if(current === "wave"){
      // zero set ξ₁²=ξ₂² i.e. lines ξ₁=±ξ₂
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:cx-R,y1:cy-R,x2:cx+R,y2:cy+R,stroke:"var(--pink)","stroke-width":2,opacity:0.85}));
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:cx-R,y1:cy+R,x2:cx+R,y2:cy-R,stroke:"var(--pink)","stroke-width":2,opacity:0.85}));
      const t = SVG("text",{x:cx,y:cy+R+18,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":11,fill:"var(--pink)"});
      t.textContent = "zero set: light-cone";
      svg.appendChild(t);
    }

    // legend
    const lg = SVG("g",{transform:"translate(540,60)"});
    svg.appendChild(lg);
    const tit = SVG("text",{x:0,y:0,"font-size":13,fill:"var(--ink)","font-weight":600});
    tit.textContent = "principal symbol σ(ξ)";
    lg.appendChild(tit);
    const desc = SVG("text",{x:0,y:24,"font-size":12,fill:"var(--mute)"});
    if(current === "laplace") desc.textContent = "−Δ : σ = ξ₁²+ξ₂²";
    else if(current === "dbar") desc.textContent = "∂̄ : σ = (i/2)(ξ₁+iξ₂)";
    else desc.textContent = "wave : σ = ξ₁²−ξ₂² (signed)";
    lg.appendChild(desc);

    const stat = SVG("text",{x:0,y:50,"font-size":12,fill:"var(--yellow)"});
    if(current === "laplace") stat.textContent = "elliptic (zero set is {0})";
    else if(current === "dbar") stat.textContent = "elliptic (zero set is {0})";
    else stat.textContent = "hyperbolic (zero set has codim 1)";
    lg.appendChild(stat);
  }

  function update(){
    draw();
    if(current === "laplace"){
      readout.textContent = "−Δ : σ₂(ξ)=|ξ|² — vanishes only at the origin, invertible elsewhere. Zero set in T*ₓM is the single point {0}, the canonical signature of ellipticity.";
    } else if(current === "dbar"){
      readout.textContent = "∂̄ : σ(ξ)=(i/2)(ξ₁+iξ₂) — zero only at ξ=0. Cauchy–Riemann is an order-1 elliptic operator on ℝ²; its kernel locally is the holomorphic functions.";
    } else {
      readout.textContent = "Wave : σ₂(τ,ξ)=−τ²+|ξ|² — vanishes on the entire light-cone. The zero set is codimension-1, so the operator is hyperbolic, not elliptic, and Atiyah–Singer does not apply.";
    }
  }
  buttons.forEach(b => b.addEventListener("click", () => {
    buttons.forEach(x => { x.classList.remove("active"); x.setAttribute("aria-pressed","false"); });
    b.classList.add("active"); b.setAttribute("aria-pressed","true");
    current = b.dataset.key;
    update();
  }));
  update();
})();

/* =============================== SECTION 3: ch · Td bar chart =============================== */
(function(){
  const svg = $("#ct-svg");
  if(!svg) return;
  const dimSlide = $("#ct-d");
  const rkSlide = $("#ct-r");
  const readout = $("#ct-readout");

  // Symbolic-ish: integer numerator / denominator for Td and ch coefficients up to deg 4
  // We display the coefficients abstractly.
  const tdCoefs = [
    {deg:0, label:"1", val:1},
    {deg:2, label:"½ c₁(TM)", val:0.5},
    {deg:4, label:"1/12 (c₁²+c₂)", val:1/12},
    {deg:6, label:"1/24 c₁ c₂", val:1/24},
    {deg:8, label:"−(1/720)(c₁⁴+...)", val:1/720}
  ];
  const chCoefs = (rk) => [
    {deg:0, label:"rk = "+rk, val:rk},
    {deg:2, label:"c₁(E)", val:1},
    {deg:4, label:"½(c₁²−2c₂)", val:0.5},
    {deg:6, label:"1/6 (c₁³−...)", val:1/6},
    {deg:8, label:"1/24 (...)", val:1/24}
  ];

  function draw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const dC = parseInt(dimSlide.value); // dim_C
    const rk = parseInt(rkSlide.value);
    const topDeg = 2*dC; // real cohomology degree we need to integrate

    const W=820, H=280;
    const baseY = 220;
    const barW = 80;
    const gap = 30;
    const x0 = 80;

    // header
    const h = SVG("text",{x:W/2,y:30,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":14,fill:"var(--ink)"});
    h.textContent = `ch(E)·Td(TM_ℂ) on M with dim_ℂ M = ${dC}, rk E = ${rk}`;
    svg.appendChild(h);
    const sub = SVG("text",{x:W/2,y:50,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":12,fill:"var(--mute)"});
    sub.textContent = `integrate the degree-${topDeg} component over [M]`;
    svg.appendChild(sub);

    // bars
    const ch = chCoefs(rk);
    for(let i=0; i<5; i++){
      const td = tdCoefs[i];
      const c = ch[i];
      const deg = td.deg;
      const x = x0 + i*(barW+gap);
      const isTop = (deg === topDeg);
      // td bar
      const tdH = Math.min(140, Math.abs(td.val)*120);
      svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{
        x:x, y:baseY-tdH, width:barW/2-2, height:tdH,
        fill:isTop ? "var(--yellow)" : "var(--violet)", opacity: isTop ? 0.95 : 0.55
      }));
      const chH = Math.min(140, Math.abs(c.val)*60);
      svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{
        x:x+barW/2+2, y:baseY-chH, width:barW/2-2, height:chH,
        fill:isTop ? "var(--yellow)" : "var(--cyan)", opacity: isTop ? 0.95 : 0.55
      }));
      // labels
      const dlab = SVG("text",{x:x+barW/2,y:baseY+18,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":11,fill:isTop?"var(--yellow)":"var(--mute)","font-weight":isTop?600:400});
      dlab.textContent = `H^${deg}`;
      svg.appendChild(dlab);
      const tdLab = SVG("text",{x:x+barW/4,y:baseY+34,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":10,fill:"var(--mute)"});
      tdLab.textContent = "Td";
      svg.appendChild(tdLab);
      const chLab = SVG("text",{x:x+3*barW/4+2,y:baseY+34,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":10,fill:"var(--mute)"});
      chLab.textContent = "ch";
      svg.appendChild(chLab);
    }
    // arrow to top-degree
    const idx = topDeg/2;
    if(idx >= 0 && idx <= 4){
      const x = x0 + idx*(barW+gap) + barW/2;
      drawArrow(svg,[x,80],[x,baseY-150],{color:"var(--yellow)",label:"the index integrand",labelSide:0,markerId:"ct-arr"});
    }
  }

  function update(){
    draw();
    const dC = parseInt(dimSlide.value);
    const rk = parseInt(rkSlide.value);
    if(dC === 1){
      readout.textContent = `dim_ℂ M = 1 (curve), rk E = ${rk}: integrand is [ch(E)·Td(TX)]_2 = c₁(E) + (rk/2) c₁(TX). For a curve of genus g and L of degree d: χ(L)=d+1-g — Riemann–Roch.`;
    } else if(dC === 2){
      readout.textContent = `dim_ℂ M = 2 (surface), rk E = ${rk}: integrand is [ch(E)·Td(TM_ℂ)]_4 = (rk/12)(c₁(TM)²+c₂(TM)) + (1/2) c₁(E) c₁(TM) + (1/2) c₁(E)² + ch₂(E). Pair with [M] for χ(X,E).`;
    } else {
      readout.textContent = `dim_ℂ M = ${dC}: the relevant integrand is the degree-${2*dC} component of ch(E)·Td(TM_ℂ). Higher-degree pieces have rational coefficients; the integer index emerges from the integral.`;
    }
  }
  dimSlide.addEventListener("input", update);
  rkSlide.addEventListener("input", update);
  update();
})();

/* =============================== SECTION 4: Special-cases dictionary =============================== */
(function(){
  const svg = $("#cs-svg");
  if(!svg) return;
  const buttons = $$(".cs-pick");
  const readout = $("#cs-readout");
  let current = "gb";

  const data = {
    gb: {
      op: "D = d + d* : Ω^ev → Ω^odd",
      ind: "ind(D) = χ(M)",
      top: "∫_M e(TM)",
      detail: "Gauss–Bonnet–Chern: χ(M) = ∫_M Pf(R/2π) — in dim 2, this is ∫ K dA / (2π).",

    },
    sig: {
      op: "D₊ : Ω₊ → Ω₋  (signature)",
      ind: "ind(D₊) = σ(M)",

    },

      op: "∂̄_E : Ω^{0,ev}(E) → Ω^{0,odd}(E)",

      t1.textContent = b.label;

      const t2 = SVG("text",{x:b.x+b.w/2,y:140,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":13,fill:d.color,"font-weight":600});
      t2.textContent = b.text;
      svg.appendChild(t2);
    }
    // = signs between boxes
    const eq1 = SVG("text",{x:282,y:138,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":24,fill:"var(--mute)"});
    eq1.textContent = "↦";
    svg.appendChild(eq1);
    const eq2 = SVG("text",{x:507,y:138,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":24,fill:"var(--mute)"});
    eq2.textContent = "=";
    svg.appendChild(eq2);
    // detail row
    const dt = SVG("text",{x:W/2,y:230,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":12,fill:"var(--ink)","font-style":"italic"});
    dt.textContent = d.detail;
    svg.appendChild(dt);
    // header
    const h = SVG("text",{x:W/2,y:50,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":14,fill:"var(--ink)","font-weight":600});
    h.textContent = "Atiyah–Singer ⟹ classical theorem";
    svg.appendChild(h);
  }

  function update(){
    draw();
    const d = data[current];
    if(current === "gb"){
      readout.textContent = "d+d* on a closed oriented manifold: ind = χ(M), integrand = e(TM). In dim 2, χ = (1/2π) ∫ K dA — Gauss–Bonnet.";
    } else if(current === "sig"){
      readout.textContent = "Signature operator on a 4k-manifold: ind = σ(M), integrand = L(TM). In dim 4, σ = (1/3) p₁[M].";
    } else {
      readout.textContent = "∂̄_E on a complex manifold: ind = χ(X,E) = Σ(−1)^k h^k(X,E), integrand = ch(E)·Td(TX). For a curve: χ(L) = d+1−g.";
    }
  }
  buttons.forEach(b => b.addEventListener("click", () => {
    buttons.forEach(x => { x.classList.remove("active"); x.setAttribute("aria-pressed","false"); });
    b.classList.add("active"); b.setAttribute("aria-pressed","true");
    current = b.dataset.key;
    update();
  }));
  update();
})();

/* =============================== SECTION 5: Dirac eigenvalues on round sphere =============================== */
(function(){
  const svg = $("#dr-svg");
  if(!svg) return;
  const rSlide = $("#dr-r");
  const buttons = $$(".dr-pick");
  const readout = $("#dr-readout");
  let n = 2;

  function draw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const W=820, H=280;
    const cy = 140;
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:60,y1:cy,x2:760,y2:cy,stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":1}));
    // 0 axis vertical
    const cx = 410;
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:cx,y1:30,x2:cx,y2:250,stroke:"var(--mute)","stroke-width":1,opacity:0.4}));

    const r = parseFloat(rSlide.value);
    // eigenvalues on unit S^n: ±(k+n/2), k=0,1,2,...
    // multiplicity ~ binom(n+k, n-1)*something ; we use simple weights
    const Kmax = 6;
    for(let k=0; k<=Kmax; k++){
      const lam = (k + n/2) / r;
      const mult = (k+1) * (n>=3 ? (k+2) : 1); // rough multiplicities
      const x = cx + Math.min(330, lam*40);
      const xn = cx - Math.min(330, lam*40);
      // size of dot encodes multiplicity
      const radius = 3 + Math.min(8, Math.sqrt(mult));
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:x,cy,r:radius,fill:"var(--cyan)",opacity:0.85}));
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:xn,cy,r:radius,fill:"var(--pink)",opacity:0.85}));
      // labels for first few
      if(k <= 3){
        const lab = SVG("text",{x:x,y:cy-radius-4,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":10,fill:"var(--mute)"});
        lab.textContent = "+(k=" + k + ")";
        svg.appendChild(lab);
        const lab2 = SVG("text",{x:xn,y:cy+radius+12,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":10,fill:"var(--mute)"});
        lab2.textContent = "−(k=" + k + ")";
        svg.appendChild(lab2);
      }
    }

    // labels
    const t1 = SVG("text",{x:cx,y:48,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":13,fill:"var(--ink)","font-weight":600});
    t1.textContent = `Dirac spectrum on S^${n}, r=${r.toFixed(2)}`;
    svg.appendChild(t1);
    const t2 = SVG("text",{x:cx,y:68,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)"});
    t2.textContent = "λ_k = ±(k + n/2)/r — symmetric, no zero modes";
    svg.appendChild(t2);
    // index box
    const box = SVG("rect",{x:600,y:200,width:160,height:50,rx:8,fill:"var(--panel2)",stroke:"var(--green)"});
    svg.appendChild(box);
    const bl = SVG("text",{x:680,y:222,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":12,fill:"var(--mute)"});
    bl.textContent = "ind D";
    svg.appendChild(bl);
    const bv = SVG("text",{x:680,y:242,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":18,fill:"var(--green)","font-weight":600});
    bv.textContent = "0";
    svg.appendChild(bv);
  }

  function update(){
    draw();
    const r = parseFloat(rSlide.value);
    readout.textContent = `D on S^${n} at r=${r.toFixed(2)}: eigenvalues ±(k+${n}/2)/r for k≥0. Spectrum is symmetric about 0, no zero-modes — so ind(D)=0, consistent with Â(S^${n})=0.`;
  }
  rSlide.addEventListener("input", update);
  buttons.forEach(b => b.addEventListener("click", () => {
    buttons.forEach(x => { x.classList.remove("active"); x.setAttribute("aria-pressed","false"); });
    b.classList.add("active"); b.setAttribute("aria-pressed","true");
    n = parseInt(b.dataset.key);
    update();
  }));
  update();
})();

/* =============================== SECTION 6: Anomaly / instanton diagram =============================== */
(function(){
  const svg = $("#an-svg");
  if(!svg) return;
  const kSlide = $("#an-k");
  const readout = $("#an-readout");

  function draw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const W=820, H=280;
    const k = parseInt(kSlide.value);

    // two columns: D_A^+ (left), D_A^- (right)
    const colXs = [220, 600];
    const labels = ["ker D_A^+", "ker D_A^−"];
    const colors = ["var(--cyan)", "var(--pink)"];
    // Dirac zero modes: |k| extra modes on side determined by sign(k)
    const counts = k >= 0 ? [k, 0] : [0, -k];

    // baseline + boxes
    for(let c=0; c<2; c++){
      svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:colXs[c]-100,y:60,width:200,height:180,rx:10,
        fill:"var(--panel2)",stroke:colors[c],"stroke-width":1.5}));
      const lab = SVG("text",{x:colXs[c],y:84,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":13,fill:colors[c],"font-weight":600});
      lab.textContent = labels[c];
      svg.appendChild(lab);
      const sub = SVG("text",{x:colXs[c],y:104,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)"});
      sub.textContent = "chiral spinors";
      svg.appendChild(sub);

      // draw zero modes as horizontal lines
      const cnt = counts[c];
      for(let i=0;i<cnt;i++){
        const y = 230 - i*22;
        svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{
          x1: colXs[c]-70, y1: y, x2: colXs[c]+70, y2: y,
          stroke: colors[c], "stroke-width":3
        }));
      }
      // count badge
      const b = SVG("text",{x:colXs[c],y:225,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":22,fill:colors[c],"font-weight":700,opacity: cnt>0 ? 0.0 : 0.6});
      b.textContent = cnt === 0 ? "(no zero modes)" : "";
      if(cnt === 0) svg.appendChild(b);
    }

    // arrow showing imbalance
    const cy = 150;
    drawArrow(svg, [330, cy], [490, cy], {color:"var(--yellow)", label:"chiral imbalance = ind(D_A) = "+(k>=0?"+":"")+k, markerId:"an-arr"});

    // bottom equation
    const eq = SVG("text",{x:W/2,y:268,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":12,fill:"var(--ink)"});
    eq.textContent = "ind(D_A) = ∫_M Â(TM)·ch(V) ≈ ∫_M c₂(V) on a 4-manifold = instanton number";
    svg.appendChild(eq);
    // header
    const h = SVG("text",{x:W/2,y:36,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":14,fill:"var(--ink)","font-weight":600});
    h.textContent = "Dirac zero-modes vs gauge instanton number k";
    svg.appendChild(h);
  }

  function update(){
    draw();
    const k = parseInt(kSlide.value);
    if(k === 0){
      readout.textContent = "k=0: trivial gauge bundle, no zero-modes, ind(D_A) = 0. The chiral anomaly is silent — left- and right-handed fermions are balanced.";
    } else {
      readout.textContent = `k=${k}: ${Math.abs(k)} extra zero-mode${Math.abs(k)===1?"":"s"} on the ${k>0?"left":"right"} side of the chiral Dirac operator. ind(D_A) = ${k>=0?"+":""}${k} = ∫_M c₂(V). The anomaly equation ∂_μ j₅^μ = −(1/8π²) tr(F∧F) integrates to this index.`;
    }
  }
  kSlide.addEventListener("input", update);
  update();
})();
</script>
